examples.title: Requirement Elicitation Process Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for conducting requirement elicitation interviews
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ElicitRequirements(stakeholders)
BEGIN
 PREPARE: Create list of questions, objectives, agenda
 FOR EACH stakeholder IN stakeholders DO
 CONDUCT interview
 RECORD responses
 CLARIFY ambiguities immediately
 ASK follow-up questions
 TAKE notes or record (with permission)
 END FOR
 ANALYZE: Compile all responses
 IDENTIFY common requirements
 RESOLVE conflicts through negotiation
 DOCUMENT requirements in formal format
 VALIDATE with stakeholders
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the step-by-step process of conducting requirement elicitation through interviews, including preparation, execution, analysis, and validation phases.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of stakeholders
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = number of requirements gathered

examples.title: SRS Review Checklist Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm for validating SRS quality against IEEE 830 standards
examples.code:
FUNCTION ValidateSRS(srsDocument)
BEGIN
 checklist = [Correctness, Unambiguity, Completeness, 
 Consistency, Verifiability, Modifiability, Traceability]
 results = EMPTY list
 
 FOR EACH criterion IN checklist DO
 score = ASSESS(criterion, srsDocument)
 IF score < threshold THEN
 ADD issues TO results
 FLAG for revision
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 GENERATE review report with results
 IF results IS empty THEN
 RETURN "SRS Approved"
 ELSE
 RETURN "Revisions Required"
 END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION ASSESS(criterion, document)
BEGIN
 SWITCH criterion
 CASE Correctness: return verifyAccurate(document)
 CASE Unambiguity: return verifyClear(document)
 CASE Completeness: return verifyAllPresent(document)
 CASE Consistency: return verifyNoConflicts(document)
 CASE Verifiability: return verifyTestable(document)
 CASE Modifiability: return verifyOrganized(document)
 CASE Traceability: return verifyLinked(document)
 END SWITCH
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how SRS validation can be systematically performed against all seven quality attributes defined in IEEE 830 standard.
examples.timeComplexity: O(c * r) where c = criteria, r = requirements

examples.title: Requirements Prioritization using MoSCoW Method
examples.description: Algorithm to categorize requirements based on business value
examples.code:
PROCEDURE PrioritizeRequirements(requirementsList, stakeholders)
BEGIN
 SET mustHave = EMPTY
 SET shouldHave = EMPTY
 SET couldHave = EMPTY
 SET wantButWillNot = EMPTY
 
 FOR EACH req IN requirementsList DO
 IMPACT = calculateBusinessImpact(req, stakeholders)
 RISK = calculateRiskIfNotDone(req)
 COST = estimateImplementationCost(req)
 PRIORITY_SCORE = (IMPACT + RISK) / COST
 
 IF PRIORITY_SCORE >= 0.8 THEN
 ADD req TO mustHave
 ELSE IF PRIORITY_SCORE >= 0.6 THEN
 ADD req TO shouldHave
 ELSE IF PRIORITY_SCORE >= 0.4 THEN
 ADD req TO couldHave
 ELSE
 ADD req TO wantButWillNot
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN {mustHave, shouldHave, couldHave, wantButWillNot}
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements the MoSCoW prioritization technique (Must, Should, Could, Won't) to help project managers decide which requirements to include given constraints.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for sorting by priority
